summary:
The process includes using living components such as cells and bioactive materials. Acellular dermis does not qualify due to its limited functional contribution versus other options that involve direct cell therapy or specific growth factors.
Active tissue engineering requires incorporation of living elements with high functionality. Acellular dermals lack sufficient active participation needed by definition, distinguishing them among provided choices. Summary:

Active tissue engineering incorporates living cells and bioactives. Acellular dermal matrices fall short because they don’t contribute significantly via active mechanisms seen in other options.
